report.summary:
The "complete" claim does NOT hold up. Catalog parity is good (en/zh-Hans/ja each at 2,623 key lines, confirmed equal), but I verified multiple genuine, user-facing defects in live code paths — this is not noise.

TWO real HIGH de-brand bugs in migrated catalog values, both rendered to users in all 3 locales: (1) cli.error.headlessNotSupportedWithSsh (en.ts:2162) tells users to use "claude ssh" — a command that does not exist; package.json bin is deepcode/deep-code and the runtime usage string says "deepcode ssh". Rendered to stderr at main.tsx:684. (2) error.ssl.certHint (en.ts:703) tells users to allowlist *.anthropic.com — wrong host for a DeepSeek product and leftover Anthropic branding; rendered via errors.ts:296. Both mirrored verbatim in zh-Hans.ts:2167/708 and ja.ts:2167/708.

Eight-plus real migration GAPS (raw literals in live components, several with catalog keys that already exist but were never wired): ShowInIDEPrompt.tsx has ZERO i18n yet is rendered by FilePermissionDialog.tsx:163 (4 of its 5 raw strings duplicate existing permission.* keys); LogSelector.tsx agentic-search strings (1317/1325/1334/1344/1421) sit beside migrated t() calls; SystemAPIErrorMessage.tsx leaks a raw timeout suffix (key exists, used by sibling) plus English "second"/"seconds" into the translated message.apiRetry; AttachmentMessage.tsx leaks English plural() nouns into translated templates; HistorySearchInput.tsx:18 ignores existing historySearch.noMatches; main.tsx:3306 raw SSH usage; Config.tsx:682 raw notifications label; errors.ts:430-455 raw PDF/image/request-size errors.

The Config.tsx terminalBell React-Compiler sentinel-guard issue is real but low practical impact (locale switch takes effect on next start). Translation QA flags (term drift for provider/agent/effort/marketplace, fullwidth-colon outliers) are mostly low priority machine-translation polish for the planned native review — EXCEPT the untranslated "leader" English leak in zh/ja permission.worker.sandboxWaitingDescription, which is a genuine wrong/English-leak bug.

Bottom line: complete on key COUNT, not on correctness or coverage. The 2 HIGH de-brand bugs and the never-wired live-component gaps (especially ShowInIDEPrompt) must be fixed before this can be called done.
mustFix: HIGH de-brand: en.ts:2162 + zh-Hans.ts:2167 + ja.ts:2167 cli.error.headlessNotSupportedWithSsh — change 'claude ssh' to 'deepcode ssh' (rendered to stderr at main.tsx:684; current text names a nonexistent command).

HIGH de-brand: en.ts:703 + zh-Hans.ts:708 + ja.ts:708 error.ssl.certHint — replace '*.anthropic.com' with the DeepSeek API domain (e.g. *.deepseek.com); leftover Anthropic branding + wrong operational guidance, rendered via errors.ts:296.

Wire ShowInIDEPrompt.tsx (currently zero i18n, live via FilePermissionDialog.tsx:163): use existing keys permission.fileEdit.question, permission.fileDialog.symlinkOutsideCwdWarning/symlinkTarget/escToCancel/tabToAmend; add new keys for 'Opened changes in {ideName} ⧉' and 'Save file to continue…' (neither exists in catalog).

LogSelector.tsx agentic-search strings (lines 1317/1325/1334/1344/1421) — add catalog keys and wire 'Searching…', 'Deep Code found these results:', 'No matching sessions found.', 'Searching with Deep Code…' (currently raw beside migrated t('logSelector.footer.*')).

SystemAPIErrorMessage.tsx — line 114 use existing messages.assistant.apiTimeoutEnvHint instead of raw '· API_TIMEOUT_MS=...' suffix; line 107 replace English 'second'/'seconds' with locale unit keys (follow stats.unit.* convention) so the translated message.apiRetry isn't mixed-language.

AttachmentMessage.tsx (lines 123/208/218/228/338) — English plural() nouns ('skill'/'type'/'teammate') leak into translated templates; add localizable unit keys so non-English locales aren't mixed-language.
HistorySearchInput.tsx:18 — wire existing historySearch.noMatches and add a key for 'search prompts:' instead of the two raw label branches.
main.tsx:3306 — migrate the raw multi-line 'Usage: deepcode ssh ...' stderr message (de-branded but unmigrated, same class as sibling migrated CLI errors); not in catalog.
Config.tsx:682 raw notifications label and errors.ts:430-455 raw PDF/image/request-size error literals — migrate to catalog keys (both are user-facing literals left raw alongside migrated siblings).

Translation leak (not awkwardness): zh-Hans/ja permission.worker.sandboxWaitingDescription leaves English 'leader' untranslated and inconsistent with permission.worker.waitingForApproval (团队负责人 / チームリーダー) — translate it.

correctnessHigh.detail: De-branding miss in a MIGRATED catalog value. en.ts:2162 = 'Error: headless (-p/--print) mode is not supported with claude ssh'. This is rendered to stderr via getMessage('cli.error.headlessNotSupportedWithSsh') at main.tsx:684 (genuine user-facing error path, not a comment). The actual command in this product is `deepcode ssh` — confirmed by package.json bin ('deepcode'/'deep-code', no 'claude') and the runtime usage string at main.tsx:3306 ('Usage: deepcode ssh ...'). The user-facing string tells users to use a command name ('claude ssh') that does not exist in this CLI, so it is both a de-brand failure and functionally misleading. The same miss is mirrored in zh-Hans.ts:2167 and ja.ts:2167 (both keep 'claude ssh'), so it is a consistent error across all three locales. Fix: change 'claude ssh' -> 'deepcode ssh' in en.ts/zh-Hans.ts/ja.ts.

De-brand miss in a user-facing catalog value. en.ts line 703 reads: 'SSL certificate error ({code}). ... or ask IT to allowlist *.anthropic.com. Run /doctor for details.' The hint is rendered to the user via getSSLErrorHint() -> getMessage('error.ssl.certHint', { code }) at errors.ts:296. For a DeepSeek-based product this is both leftover Anthropic branding AND incorrect operational guidance (the host to allowlist should be the DeepSeek API domain, e.g. *.deepseek.com, not *.anthropic.com). The same literal '*.anthropic.com' is propagated verbatim into zh-Hans.ts:708 and ja.ts:708, so the de-brand defect exists in all three locales. The {code} placeholder itself matches the call site correctly; the problem is the prose content.

correctnessMed.detail: A genuine user-facing prose literal was left raw alongside the migrated getMessage() calls. main.tsx:3306 prints to stderr on the wrong-usage SSH path: 'Usage: deepcode ssh <user@host | ssh-config-alias> [dir]\n\nRuns Deep Code on a remote Linux host. You don't need to install...'. This string is NOT present in en.ts (grep for 'remote Linux host'/'ssh-config-alias'/'auth login' -> not in catalog) and is not wrapped in getMessage(), while sibling CLI error/warning strings in the same file are all migrated. It is correctly de-branded ('Deep Code', 'deepcode ssh', 'deepcode auth login'), so this is a migration-completeness gap, not a branding error. Note: the inline commander .description(...) strings at main.tsx:3302 are similarly raw, but those are commander --help descriptions (a different category — there is no command.ssh.description catalog key) and may be intentionally out of scope; flagging 3306 specifically because it is a runtime stderr message in the same class as the migrated ones.

React-Compiler memoization defect in NotifChannelLabel (lines 1713-1723). The 'terminal_bell' case memoizes JSX containing {t('settings.config.notifChannel.terminalBell')} inside a compile-time-constant guard: `if ($[1] === Symbol.for("react.memo_cache_sentinel")) { t1 = <Text>{t(...)} ...</Text>; $[1] = t1; }`. The original code memoized a literal constant ('Terminal Bell'), so a no-dependency sentinel guard was correct. The migration (commit 43d201b, stated policy 'in-place t() ... no _c()/guard changes') substituted a t()-derived value into that constant slot without converting it to a t-dependency guard. Result: the translated string is baked in on first render and never re-translates if the UI locale changes at runtime (the same Config panel has a uiLanguage row at lines 663-684 that calls setActiveLocale). Sibling cases auto/iterm2WithBell/disabled use `return t(...)` directly and DO update, creating an inconsistent stale branch. Not a crash (t identity is stable if memoized by the provider) and the project documents full locale switch as 'takes effect on next start', which limits practical impact — hence medium, not high. Correct fix: let the compiler regenerate the guard (e.g. `if ($[0] !== t)`), or move this case to a direct `return <Text>{t(...)} ...</Text>` like the other t()-based cases.

Genuine user-facing literal left raw alongside migrated ones (criterion #5). Line 682: `label: feature('KAIROS') || feature('KAIROS_PUSH_NOTIFICATION') ? 'Local notifications' : 'Notifications',`. Both branches are user-facing English strings with no t()/getMessage() wrapping — verified to be the ONLY raw label in the file (all other 50+ labels use t()). The migration commit 43d201b explicitly claims it handled 'Settings/Config.tsx (the ~33 raw labels the prior partial migration missed)', so this row is a miss within the migration's own declared scope, not merely pre-existing/out-of-scope (git confirms this commit did not touch line 682). It renders English even under zh-Hans/ja locales while neighboring rows localize. Fix: add keys settings.config.notifications and settings.config.localNotifications to en/zh-Hans/ja and wrap with t().

Genuine user-facing literals left raw alongside migrated ones in the SAME file. The i18n commit 43d201b migrated 16 runtime/api/ssl error strings to getMessage(), but left 6 user-facing English prose strings un-migrated at errors.ts:427-457: getPdfTooLargeErrorMessage ('PDF too large (...). Double press esc to go back ...'), getPdfPasswordProtectedErrorMessage ('PDF is password protected. ...'), getPdfInvalidErrorMessage ('The PDF file was not valid. ...'), getImageTooLargeErrorMessage ('Image was too large. ...'), getRequestTooLargeErrorMessage ('Request too large (...). ...'). No catalog keys exist for any of these (grep of en.ts for error.pdf/error.image/error.request and the literal text returns nothing). These are clearly end-user messages (esp. the interactive 'Double press esc' variants) and were skipped by the migration, so non-English users will see English here while neighboring runtime errors are localized. Inconsistent/incomplete migration.

The footer Byline at line 128 leaves three genuine user-facing strings RAW (unmigrated) while the rest of the file is migrated: <KeyboardShortcutHint shortcut="s/Enter" action="save" />, <KeyboardShortcutHint shortcut="e" action="edit in your editor" />, and <ConfigurableShortcutHint ... description="cancel" />. These props ARE rendered verbatim as user-facing text (KeyboardShortcutHint renders `action` directly e.g. 'Enter to confirm'; ConfigurableShortcutHint passes `description` straight into KeyboardShortcutHint's `action`). The components fully support translation — other migrated files pass t()/getMessage() into them: DeepSeekSetupDialog.tsx (action={t('setup.deepseek.hint.continue')}, description={t('setup.deepseek.hint.cancel')}), LogSelector.tsx, SessionPreview.tsx, SessionBackgroundHint.tsx, permissions/rules/AddWorkspaceDirectory.tsx (description={getMessage('permission.addWorkspaceDir.footer.cancel')}). So the rendered footer 's/Enter to save · e to edit in your editor · Esc to cancel' stays English in zh-Hans/ja. Mitigating: ALL sibling wizard-steps in the same new-agent-creation directory (MemoryStep, ToolsStep, TypeStep, MethodStep, ColorStep, ModelStep, PromptStep, GenerateStep, LocationStep, DescriptionStep) also leave these footer hints raw, so this is a systemic scoping gap of the wizard-footer layer, not a defect unique to this file. Scored medium (criterion 5: genuine user-facing literals left raw alongside migrated ones), not high, because the migrated keys themselves are all correct and the omission is consistent across the whole subtree.

Line 409: `<Text bold={true}>claude.ai</Text>` is a raw, un-migrated user-facing section heading rendered directly alongside fully migrated sibling scope headings (e.g. `{t('mcp.list.scope.agent')}` on line 418, `{dynamicHeading.label}` on line 427, and all `getScopeHeading()` labels which use `getMessage('mcp.list.scope.*')`). This violates criterion 5 (genuine user-facing literal left raw alongside migrated ones). It is also a brand name surfacing in an otherwise de-branded ('Deep Code'/'DeepSeek') UI. The label corresponds to the `claudeai-proxy` config type (see _temp2/_temp at lines 502/505). Either it should have been migrated to a catalog key (e.g. `mcp.list.scope.claudeAi`) like every other group heading, or intentionally kept as a provider identifier — but as-is it is an inconsistent, non-localizable, branded literal. Note: this is a literal in the .tsx, NOT a catalog value; no `mcp.list.*` catalog value contains 'Claude'/'Anthropic'.

Genuine user-facing English literal left raw alongside migrated siblings. The 'vscode-command-install' tip returns a hardcoded template literal: `Open the Command Palette (Cmd+Shift+P) and run "Shell Command: Install '${...}' command in PATH" to enable IDE integration`. There is NO tip.vscode-command-install.content key in en.ts (or zh-Hans.ts/ja.ts) — confirmed absent in all three catalogs. Every other user-facing tip in the same externalTips array WAS migrated to getMessage('tip.*.content'), including the adjacent IDE tip 'ide-upsell-external-terminal' (line 281). This is an incomplete/inconsistent migration: this tip will always render in English regardless of locale. The dynamic env.terminal segment could be passed as a {terminal} placeholder param. This is the only user-facing literal left un-migrated.

qaFlags.issue: (c) Inconsistent terminology for "provider". This entry (and command.doctor.description) renders provider as 服务商, but all seven /provider runtime messages (command.provider.current/invalid/legacyOnly/set/setWithBaseUrl/usage) use 提供商. Same group must pick one term.
qaFlags.suggestion: Standardize on one term across the group. Recommend 服务商 everywhere (more idiomatic), i.e. change the command.provider.* messages from 提供商 to 服务商; or, if keeping 提供商 in messages, change this to 切换模型提供商（…）.

qaFlags.issue: (c) Inconsistent terminology for "agent". Here "agent" is left in English (管理 agent 配置) but command.reloadPlugins.result.count.agent translates it as 代理. JA is consistent (both エージェント); only ZH disagrees. 代理 also reads as proxy, a poor fit for AI agents.
qaFlags.suggestion: Use one term across the group. Recommend keeping English "agent" (or 智能体) in both: change command.reloadPlugins.result.count.agent from 代理 to agent (or 智能体) to match. Avoid 代理 for AI agents.

qaFlags.issue: (c) Inconsistent + awkward terminology for "effort". This renders effort as 投入等级 (投入 = investment/input, awkward for AI effort), while command.effort.* messages keep the English word "effort" (e.g. "effort 级别") and command.model.effortInfoSuffix/withEffortSuffix render it as 推理强度. Three different renderings for one term.
qaFlags.en: Set effort level for model usage
qaFlags.translated: 设置模型使用的投入等级
qaFlags.suggestion: Pick one rendering across the group. Recommend 推理强度 (already used in command.model.*) — e.g. 设置模型的推理强度 — and align the command.effort.* messages, instead of the awkward 投入等级.

qaFlags.issue: (b)/(c) Awkward + inconsistent rendering of "effort". 労力 (manual toil/labor) is an unnatural word for model reasoning effort. Also inconsistent with the group: command.effort.* messages keep English "effort" (e.g. "effort レベル") and command.model.effortInfoSuffix/withEffortSuffix use 推論強度.

qaFlags.translated: モデル使用時の労力レベルを設定します
qaFlags.suggestion: Use a consistent, natural term. Recommend 推論強度 (already used in command.model.*): モデルの推論強度を設定します, and align the command.effort.* entries. Avoid 労力.

qaFlags.issue: (c) Inconsistent rendering of "brief mode". command.brief.description translates it as 精简模式, but command.brief.enabled/disabled render it as 仅 Brief 模式 (keeping English "Brief") and command.brief.notEntitled uses Brief 工具. Same feature, three different names.
qaFlags.translated: 已启用仅 Brief 模式
qaFlags.suggestion: Unify with the description's 精简模式: 已启用仅精简模式 / 已禁用仅精简模式, and update command.brief.notEntitled accordingly (e.g. 精简工具) — or align the description to whatever single term is chosen.

qaFlags.issue: (c) Inconsistent rendering of "pull request". This translates it as 拉取请求, but command.review.description and command.prComments.description keep English "pull request" and command.commitPushPr.description uses PR. 拉取请求 is also uncommon in Chinese dev UIs (PR / pull request is standard).

qaFlags.issue: (b)/(c) Punctuation inconsistency. This (and command.keybindings.editorError, command.logout.clearConfigError, command.model.validationFailed, command.color.set) uses a fullwidth colon "：" before the value, but ~59 sibling JA messages use halfwidth colon + space ": " before {error}/{message} (e.g. command.advisor.invalidModel, command.effort.failedToSet). The fullwidth-colon entries are the outliers.
qaFlags.translated: ヒープダンプの作成に失敗しました：{error}
qaFlags.suggestion: Use halfwidth colon + space to match the group: ヒープダンプの作成に失敗しました: {error}. Apply the same fix to the other fullwidth-colon failure messages listed.

qaFlags.issue: (c) Inconsistent term casing for "advisor" within the advisor.* cluster. Here and in several messages the English word "Advisor" is kept (Advisor はすでに未設定です), while command.advisor.description, command.advisor.cannotUse, command.advisor.invalidModel, and command.advisor.setUnsupported use the translated アドバイザー. Mixing English "Advisor" and アドバイザー in body text is inconsistent.
qaFlags.translated: Advisor はすでに未設定です。
qaFlags.suggestion: Standardize body-text references to アドバイザー (アドバイザーはすでに未設定です。). Keeping "Advisor:" only as a fixed label prefix (command.advisor.current/notSet/set) is acceptable, but non-label sentences should use アドバイザー consistently.

qaFlags.issue: "leader" left untranslated, and inconsistent with the same role rendered as "团队负责人" in permission.worker.waitingForApproval ("等待团队负责人批准"). "leader" should be Chinese.

Word order is off for a native speaker; also inconsistent with the parallel permission.prompt.rejectFeedbackPlaceholder ("应如何改进") and permission.computerUse.optionDeny ("该如何调整"). qaFlags.issue: (b) awkward as a tab label. EN 'Discover' is the name of a tab where users explore/find new plugins. '发现' reads like a passive 'found/discovered' notification rather than an action tab; the matching status string plugin.discover.title uses '发现插件' (verb) but the bare tab '发现' is weak/awkward.

qaFlags.issue: (c) terminology inconsistency. This tab is left fully English 'Marketplaces' while elsewhere the same concept is translated as '应用市场' (plugin.browse.*) and '市场' (plugin.discover.*/plugin.op.*). The zh file uses three different renderings of 'marketplace' (English 'marketplace', '应用市场', '市场') plus English 'Marketplaces' — pick one consistent term.
qaFlags.suggestion: 统一术语：建议全部用 '市场'（或全部保留英文 'Marketplace'），此处改为 '市场'。当前 plugin.* 命名空间内 marketplace 被译成 'marketplace'/'应用市场'/'市场'/'Marketplaces' 四种写法，需统一。

qaFlags.issue: (c) terminology inconsistency within a single string. EN: 'Add a marketplace first using \'Add marketplace\'.' The translation renders marketplace twice with two different words: '请先使用 \'Add marketplace\' 添加应用市场。' — the noun uses '应用市场' but other browse keys and discover keys use '市场'. Inconsistent with the rest of the namespace.

qaFlags.suggestion: 请先使用 'Add marketplace' 添加市场。（统一为 '市场'）

qaFlags.issue: (d/c) header left as English 'Marketplaces：' while the sibling section headers are translated: installationHeader='安装：', managementHeader='管理：', validationHeader='校验：', otherHeader='其他：'. A section header in a help list should be translated to match the others.

qaFlags.issue: (e) left partially English. EN 'Plugin Command Usage:' → 'Plugin 命令用法：' keeps 'Plugin' in English even though 'plugin' is consistently translated as '插件' everywhere else in the file (e.g. plugin.tab.paneTitle='插件'). The leading word should be translated.

qaFlags.issue: (b) awkward as a tab label. EN 'Discover' is a noun-like tab name. '見つける' (a plain verb 'to find') is unnatural for a tab; product UIs use '見つける' rarely. The matching screen title plugin.discover.title='プラグインを探す' uses 探す, so the tab is also inconsistent with it.

qaFlags.issue: (c) terminology + punctuation inconsistency. EN 'By: {author}'. Here translated '作成者：{author}' (full-width colon) but the parallel key plugin.browse.details.author renders the same EN as '作者: {author}' (different word '作者' AND half-width colon). The whole ja plugin.discover.* block uses full-width colons while plugin.browse.* uses half-width — and 作者 vs 作成者 differ for identical source text.

qaFlags.issue: (d/c) header left as English 'Marketplaces:' while sibling headers are translated: installationHeader='インストール:', managementHeader='管理:', validationHeader='検証:', otherHeader='その他:'. Should be translated for consistency.
qaFlags.suggestion: マーケットプレイス:

qaFlags.issue: (e) left partially English. EN 'Plugin Command Usage:' → 'Plugin コマンドの使い方:' keeps 'Plugin' in English even though plugin is translated 'プラグイン' everywhere else (e.g. plugin.tab.paneTitle='プラグイン'). The leading word should be translated.
qaFlags.translated: Plugin コマンドの使い方:
qaFlags.suggestion: プラグイン コマンドの使い方:

qaFlags.issue: (c) terminology inconsistency for 'marketplace'. The ja file splits 'marketplace' two ways with no rule: kept English in plugin.addMarketplace.*, plugin.help.*, plugin.marketplaceList.*, plugin.errors.removedMarketplace, plugin.validate.usage; but translated 'マーケットプレイス' in plugin.browse.*, plugin.discover.*, plugin.op.*, plugin.marketplace.loadError.*. Same concept, same screens — should be one term. Flagging this representative key; the fix applies file-wide.

qaFlags.issue: (b) produces broken mixed output. The {errorWord} placeholder is filled by the English plural() helper, so it interpolates 'error'/'errors' (English). '{count} 个{errorWord}：' renders as e.g. '3 个errors：' — the Chinese measure word '个' directly precedes the English noun 'errors', which reads broken. (This is partly a code-architecture issue: plural() is not localized, so the translator cannot fully fix it here, but the '个' measure word makes it worse than a neutral phrasing.)
qaFlags.suggestion: {count} 个错误：（去掉 {errorWord} 占位符改为固定 '错误'，因为该占位符仅会注入英文 error/errors，与中文混排不自然；或在代码侧本地化 plural()）

qaFlags.issue: Composition bug producing ungrammatical Japanese. This template ("MCP サーバー \"{serverName}\" を{status}しました") is fed status = statusEnabled/statusDisabled, which are "有効"/"無効" (na-adjective/noun forms). The rendered string becomes "…を有効しました" / "…を無効しました", which is grammatically wrong — 有効/無効 cannot take する directly. (Note the sibling key bulkResult is correct because it is fed the -化 Title forms.)
qaFlags.translated: MCP サーバー "{serverName}" を{status}しました
qaFlags.suggestion: Either change the template to "MCP サーバー \"{serverName}\" を{status}に設定しました" so "有効/無効" reads as "有効に設定しました", OR (root-cause fix) make this template consume the -化 Title forms like bulkResult does, yielding "…を有効化しました" / "…を無効化しました".

qaFlags.issue: Broken word order. The UI renders A + bold(Enter) + B as "押してください Enter ブラウザで認証した後に。". Japanese is verb-final, so placing the verb 押してください first and a trailing adverbial clause after the key is clearly ungrammatical to a native reader.
qaFlags.en: Press [Enter] after authenticating in your browser.
qaFlags.translated: A="押してください "  B=" ブラウザで認証した後に。"
qaFlags.suggestion: Move the context into the A fragment and the verb into B: A="ブラウザで認証した後に ", B=" を押してください。" → renders "ブラウザで認証した後に Enter を押してください。" (The B fragment for pressEnterAfterAuthB should become " を押してください。".)

qaFlags.issue: Broken word order. Renders "押してください Enter 完了したら。" — verb-first with a trailing 完了したら clause is ungrammatical Japanese.
qaFlags.translated: A="押してください "  B=" 完了したら。"
qaFlags.suggestion: Restructure so the verb is last: A="完了したら ", B=" を押してください。" → "完了したら Enter を押してください。".

qaFlags.issue: Broken word order. Renders "押してください Enter ブラウザを開くには。" — the verb 押してください comes before the key and the purpose clause dangles at the end. Ungrammatical / unnatural Japanese.
qaFlags.translated: A="押してください "  B=" ブラウザを開くには。"
qaFlags.suggestion: Restructure: A="ブラウザを開くには ", B=" を押してください。" → "ブラウザを開くには Enter を押してください。".

qaFlags.issue: Broken / dangling word order. The UI renders A + bold(Enter) + B as "按 Enter 在浏览器中完成认证后。". The temporal clause "…后" trails after the verb+object, which reads as ungrammatical Chinese (the time clause must precede the verb).
qaFlags.en: Press [Enter] after authenticating in your browser.
qaFlags.translated: A="按 "  B=" 在浏览器中完成认证后。"
qaFlags.suggestion: Move the time clause into A and keep the verb in B: A="在浏览器中完成认证后，按 ", B=" 即可。" (or B="。") → "在浏览器中完成认证后，按 Enter 即可。".

qaFlags.issue: Broken / dangling word order. Renders "按 Enter 完成后。" — the "完成后" (when done) clause must precede the action, not follow it. Ungrammatical Chinese.
qaFlags.suggestion: Move the clause into A: A="完成后，按 ", B=" 即可。" → "完成后，按 Enter 即可。".

qaFlags.issue: Dropped brand name. English is "DeepCode needs your input" (a system notification title where the brand provides essential context for which app is asking). The translation "需要您的输入" omits DeepCode entirely, so the notification no longer identifies the source app.
qaFlags.suggestion: DeepCode 需要您的输入

qaFlags.issue: Dropped brand name. English is "DeepCode needs your input" (a notification title that should name the app). The translation "入力が必要です" drops DeepCode, losing the source-app context in the notification.
qaFlags.suggestion: DeepCode が入力を必要としています  (or 「DeepCode：入力が必要です」)

qaFlags.issue: Slight mistranslation of the preposition. English "Authenticating with {serverName}…" means authenticating against/with the server. "正在通过 {serverName} 认证" reads as authenticating *via/through* {serverName}, framing the server as an identity provider rather than the target being authenticated to.
qaFlags.suggestion: 正在向 {serverName} 进行身份验证…  (或 "正在与 {serverName} 进行认证…")

qaFlags.issue: Awkward/ungrammatical. Rendered as `↑ {scrollOffset} {moreAbove}`, so the UI shows e.g. “↑ 5 在上方” (“5 above”), which drops the “more” meaning and lacks a measure word — reads as broken Chinese. The ja counterpart “件が上にあります” reads naturally; zh should match that fullness.
qaFlags.suggestion: 项在上方  (giving “↑ 5 项在上方”), or 还有更多

qaFlags.issue: Same problem as moreAbove. Rendered as “↑ 5 在下方” — missing measure word and the “more” notion; ungrammatical fragment.
qaFlags.suggestion: 项在下方  (giving “... 5 项在下方”)

qaFlags.en: ...days to retain transcripts (default is 30)... no transcripts are written...
qaFlags.translated: ...保留转录记录的天数...不写入任何转录记录...
qaFlags.issue: Terminology inconsistency. “transcript” is rendered “转录记录” here but “会话记录” in cli.error.unableToLoadTranscript (“无法从文件加载会话记录”). Same concept (session transcript) should use one term. “转录” (transcription, as in audio) also reads slightly off for a chat log.
qaFlags.suggestion: Use 会话记录 consistently (replace 转录记录 here)

qaFlags.translated: 常に完全な応答をコピーを{state}
qaFlags.issue: Wrong/awkward grammar: double を (「…をコピーを有効」). The base label phrase already ends in 「…をコピー」, so appending 「を{state}」 yields an ungrammatical double-object string.
qaFlags.suggestion: 常に完全な応答をコピーする設定を{state}にしました  (or drop the trailing を and use コピー機能を{state}化)

qaFlags.issue: Awkward / inconsistent word order. Other toggle summaries follow “{state}+动词短语” (e.g. “{state}自动连接到 IDE”), but this reorders to a noun phrase “IDE 扩展自动安装” and adds a stray space after {state}, not matching its own label “自动安装 IDE 扩展”.
qaFlags.suggestion: {state}自动安装 IDE 扩展  (no space, verb-phrase order matching the label)

qaFlags.issue: WRONG/structural. The measure word 个 is baked into the word ('个问题'), but every consuming template already supplies count + a preceding noun, so it renders doubled measure words. In notifs.settingsErrors.found ('发现 {count} 个设置{issueLabel}') this produces '发现 5 个设置个问题', and in diagnostics.summary it produces '...3 个新诊断个问题' — '个设置个问题' / '诊断个问题' are ungrammatical.
qaFlags.suggestion: Use the bare noun '问题' (no embedded 个). Then '发现 5 个设置问题' / '...3 个新诊断问题' read correctly.

qaFlags.issue: WRONG/structural, same root cause as the singular. '个问题' as the plural label doubles the measure word in its consuming templates (e.g. '发现 5 个设置个问题').
qaFlags.suggestion: Use bare '问题'.

qaFlags.issue: WRONG/structural. The counter 件の is baked into the word ('件の問題'), but consuming templates already provide count + counter + a preceding noun, producing doubled counters: notifs.settingsErrors.found renders '5 件の設定件の問題が見つかりました' and diagnostics.summary renders '...3 件の新しい診断件の問題...'. '設定件の問題' / '診断件の問題' are ungrammatical.
qaFlags.suggestion: Use the bare noun '問題' (drop 件の). Then '5 件の設定問題' / '...3 件の新しい診断問題' read correctly.

qaFlags.issue: WRONG/structural, same root cause as the singular. '件の問題' doubles the counter inside its consuming templates.
qaFlags.suggestion: Use bare '問題'.

qaFlags.issue: Awkward/broken once composed. Template '在 {fileCount} {fileWord} 中发现 {count} 个新诊断{issueWord}' renders '在 5 个文件 中发现 3 个新诊断个问题': (a) stray space in '个文件 中' (Chinese needs no space before 中), and (b) '个新诊断个问题' is ungrammatical once {issueWord} carries its own 个.
qaFlags.translated: 在 {fileCount} {fileWord} 中发现 {count} 个新诊断{issueWord}
qaFlags.suggestion: Drop the embedded 个 from the issue word (see diagnostics.issue.*) and remove the space before 中: '在 {fileCount} {fileWord}中发现 {count} 个新诊断{issueWord}' → '在 5 个文件中发现 3 个新诊断问题'.

qaFlags.issue: Awkward/broken once composed. Renders '5 件のファイル に 3 件の新しい診断件の問題が見つかりました': '診断件の問題' is ungrammatical because {issueWord} carries its own 件の (also a stray space before に).
qaFlags.translated: {fileCount} {fileWord} に {count} 件の新しい診断{issueWord}が見つかりました
qaFlags.suggestion: Drop the embedded 件の from the issue word (see diagnostics.issue.*): '{fileCount} {fileWord}に {count} 件の新しい診断{issueWord}が見つかりました' → '5 件のファイルに 3 件の新しい診断問題が見つかりました'.

qaFlags.issue: Mistranslation in context. This labels the agents/files that consume the most context tokens, but '主要贡献者' reads as human 'main contributors' (people who contributed to the project). The Japanese (主な要因 = main factors) got the intent right.
qaFlags.suggestion: Use '└ 主要来源：' or '└ 占用最多：' (top sources / biggest consumers) to convey token contributors, not people.

qaFlags.issue: Awkward/wrong. This is the status of the search (ripgrep) subsystem ('Search: Not working'). '无法工作' reads as a person being unable to work; it is not idiomatic for a feature/subsystem being down.
qaFlags.suggestion: Use '不可用' or '无法使用' (or '工作异常'). 'Search: 不可用' reads naturally.

qaFlags.issue: Inconsistent terminology. Uses '智能体' for agent and '个 token' for the unit, whereas the directly parallel doctor key doctor.contextWarnings.agents.message keeps English 'agent' and '{tokens} tokens'. Same drift in largeMemoryFiles.warning ('{chars} 个字符' vs doctor's '{chars} 字符').
qaFlags.en: Large cumulative agent descriptions will impact performance (~{tokens} tokens > {threshold})
qaFlags.translated: 累计的智能体描述过大将影响性能（约 {tokens} 个 token > {threshold}）
qaFlags.suggestion: Align with the doctor block: use 'agent' (not 智能体) and '{tokens} tokens' (not '个 token'): '累计的 agent 描述过大将影响性能（约 {tokens} tokens > {threshold}）'.

qaFlags.issue: Minor terminology inconsistency. Uses 'エージェント' and 'トークン', while the near-identical doctor key doctor.contextWarnings.agents.message keeps English 'agent' and 'tokens'. Within the doctor/status group the two warnings disagree on the term.
qaFlags.en: Large cumulative agent descriptions will impact performance (~{tokens} tokens > {threshold})
qaFlags.translated: 累積されたエージェントの説明が大きいとパフォーマンスに影響します（約 {tokens} トークン > {threshold}）
qaFlags.suggestion: For consistency with the parallel doctor warning, use 'agent' and 'tokens': '累積された agent の説明が大きいとパフォーマンスに影響します（約 {tokens} tokens > {threshold}）' (or normalize the doctor block to エージェント/トークン instead — pick one).

qaFlags.issue: EN 'Press ' is the start of a string assembled as 'Press [key] to save'. The ja value '押す ' uses the plain dictionary verb form placed BEFORE the key, producing broken word order like '押す [Enter] で保存'. Japanese requires the verb after the object. (zh '按 ' is fine because Chinese is verb-first.)
qaFlags.suggestion: Restructure with the matching saveHint.toSave fragment so it reads naturally, e.g. set before to an empty/leading particle and use '[key] を押して保存' ordering. Practical fix for this fragment alone: change toSave to ' を押して保存、' and leave before empty, or set before to '' so the assembled sentence is '[Enter] を押して保存'.

qaFlags.issue: EN '?' (question-mark suffix for 'Are you sure you want to delete the agent [name]?'). The ja value is '：' (full-width colon), so the assembled prompt ends '…削除しますか：' which is grammatically wrong — a yes/no question must not end with a colon. (zh correctly uses ' 吗？'.)

Inconsistent terminology for 'agent' across this one namespace group: agent.validation.* renders it '智能体' (e.g. '智能体可以访问所有工具'), agents.list.*/agents.menu.* render it '代理' (e.g. '{count} 个代理', '创建新代理'), and many agents.confirm.*/agents.description.* leave it English 'agent' (e.g. '已创建 agent'). Three different terms (智能体 / 代理 / agent) for the same concept confuses users.
qaFlags.suggestion: Pick one canonical term and apply it consistently across all agent.* and agents.* keys (recommend keeping English 'agent' since the menu/wizard already mixes it and the product term is 'agent', or standardize on '智能体'). At minimum, do not use both '代理' and '智能体' in the same feature.

qaFlags.issue: Inconsistent terminology for 'agent': agent.validation.* and most agents.* use 'エージェント', but agent.editor.* and agents.confirm.* leave it English 'agent' (e.g. 'agent の保存に失敗しました', 'agent を作成しました'). Mixing 'エージェント' and 'agent' within the same namespace is inconsistent.
qaFlags.suggestion: Standardize on 'エージェント' throughout (change 'agent の保存に失敗しました' → 'エージェントの保存に失敗しました', 'agent を作成しました' → 'エージェントを作成しました', etc.) to match the rest of the namespace.

qaFlags.issue: Awkward/ungrammatical when assembled. The {verb} placeholder is filled with finite polite verb forms from the sibling keys — memSearch.searched/searchedCap = '検索しました' and memSearch.searching = '検索中' (verified in CollapsedReadSearchContent.tsx line 437-441, which passes verb_3 directly into this string). That yields '検索しましたメモリ' (a finite past-polite verb glued directly onto a noun, which is broken Japanese) and the marginal '検索中メモリ'. Unlike the memRead/memWrite lines, here the verb sits with NO separator before the noun.
qaFlags.suggestion: Restructure so the noun precedes the verb: 'メモリを{verb}' → renders '메모리を検索しました' / 'メモリを検索中'. (zh '{verb}记忆' is fine because Chinese verbs attach to nouns freely, so only ja needs changing.)

qaFlags.issue: Register inconsistency within the same rendered line. The git summary line concatenates these verbs (CollapsedReadSearchContent.tsx pushPart, e.g. 'コミットしました abc, プッシュ先 main'). Siblings committed='コミットしました', merged='マージしました', amendedCommit='コミットを修正しました' are all finite verb-past forms, but pushedTo='プッシュ先' and rebasedOnto='リベース先' switch to noun-label ('push destination'/'rebase destination') style. Mixing verb-past and noun-label in one line reads inconsistently. (en is uniformly verb-phrase: pushed to / rebased onto; zh is uniformly verb: 已推送到 / 已变基到.)
qaFlags.suggestion: Use verb-past forms to match siblings: pushedTo → 'プッシュしました', collapsedTool.git.rebasedOnto → 'リベースしました'.

qaFlags.issue: Broken composition (measure word duplicated). This value is injected as {noun} into 'message.teamMem.readPart' = "{verb} {count} 条团队{noun}" and writePart. Because readPart already contains a measure word "条" before the noun, the rendered output becomes "已回忆 3 条团队条记忆" — a duplicated "条...条" with awkward "团队条记忆". The English {noun} is the bare word "memories" with no measure word, which is why the EN template "{verb} {count} team {noun}" works.
qaFlags.suggestion: Drop the measure word from this value so it is a bare noun: set memories="记忆" (and 'message.teamMem.memory' likewise "记忆"). Then readPart renders "已回忆 3 条团队记忆" correctly. Alternatively rework readPart/writePart to not pre-include "条". Same fix needed for 'message.teamMem.memory'.

qaFlags.issue: Redundant/awkward composition. This value is injected as {noun} into 'message.teamMem.readPart' = "{count} {noun}のチームメモリを{verb}" (and writePart). Rendered output becomes "3 件のメモリのチームメモリを想起済み" — "件のメモリのチームメモリ" repeats メモリ and is unnatural. The EN {noun} is the bare word "memories" so the EN template "{verb} {count} team {noun}" reads cleanly.
qaFlags.suggestion: Make this a bare noun without the counter: set memories="メモリ" (and 'message.teamMem.memory' likewise "メモリ"), yielding "3 メモリのチームメモリを想起済み" — still slightly redundant. Better: rework readPart/writePart to "チームメモリを{count} 件{verb}" and make memory/memories empty or unused. At minimum eliminate the doubled メモリ. Same fix needed for 'message.teamMem.memory'.

qaFlags.issue: Left fully in English — user-facing leakage. Interpolated as {memoryWord} into 'systemMessage.memorySaved.count' = "{count} 条 {memoryWord}", rendering "已保存 1 条 memory". Inconsistent with the already-translated 'message.teamMem.memory'.

qaFlags.issue: Redundant/garbled output. The ja template appends "（{wasWord}）" and wasWord="破棄", so it renders as "...が破棄されました（破棄）。" — the word "破棄" appears twice in a row, which reads as a nonsensical duplication. English has no such parenthetical; {wasWord}/{wereWord} only exist to satisfy EN singular/plural verb agreement, which Japanese does not need.
qaFlags.translated: worktree を削除しました。{branch} の {count} 件の {commitWord} が破棄されました（{wasWord}）。{tmuxNote}
qaFlags.suggestion: Drop the trailing "（{wasWord}）" entirely: "worktree を削除しました。{branch} の {count} 件の {commitWord} を破棄しました。{tmuxNote}". The {wasWord}/{wereWord} placeholders are an English grammar artifact and should not be surfaced in Japanese.

qaFlags.issue: wasWord="破棄" and wereWord="破棄" are only consumed by worktree.exit.removedCommits, where they create the redundant "破棄されました（破棄）" duplication described above. As standalone values they are meaningless verb fragments in Japanese.
qaFlags.suggestion: If the removedCommits template is fixed to not append them, set both to an empty string "" (or leave them unused). They should not render as "破棄" next to the already-present "破棄されました".

qaFlags.issue: Broken word order when concatenated. The UI joins pressLabel + key + toCancel = "押してください " + "Escape" + " でキャンセル" → "押してください Escape でキャンセル", which is ungrammatical Japanese (verb "押してください" precedes its object). Japanese particle order requires the key before the verb.
qaFlags.suggestion: Make pressLabel empty/minimal and put the verb in toCancel so it renders naturally, e.g. pressLabel="" and toCancel=" を押すとキャンセルします" → "Escape を押すとキャンセルします". (zh handles this correctly: "按 " + Escape + " 取消".)
